title: Visualize distributive property
description: Practice visualizing the distributive using tape diagrams and arrays.
topic_path: Get ready for equations, expressions, and inequalities

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the array to break $7\times4$ into two groups.**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The array shows $7\times4$, or  $7$ fours.

Let's use the array to break the $7$ fours into two parts.

The dotted line breaks up the $7$ rows into $\blueD5$ rows and $\greenD2$ rows.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Above the line, there are $\blueD{5 \text{ rows of }4{ \text{ dots}}}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Below the line, there are $\greenD{2 \text{ rows of }4{ \text{ dots}}}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]
$7\times4=\blueD{5 \text{ fours}} + \greenD{2\text{ fours}}$

options.alt: An array of 28 circles arranged in 7 rows of 4 circles each. The circles in the top 5 rows are shaded blue. The circles in the bottom 2 rows are shaded green. A dotted line separates the top 5 rows from the bottom 2 rows. A curved bracket across the top of the array is labeled 4. A curved bracket on the left side of the array is labeled 7.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the array to break $8\times6$ into two groups.**

[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The array shows $8\times6$, or  $8$ sixes.

Let's use the array to break the $8$ sixes into two parts.

The dotted line breaks up the $8$ rows into $\blueD5$ rows and $\greenD3$ rows.



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Above the line, there are $\blueD{5 \text{ rows of }6{ \text{ dots}}}$. 


[[☃ image 1]]

Below the line, there are $\greenD{3 \text{ rows of }6{ \text{ dots}}}$. 



[[☃ image 1]]
$8\times6=\blueD{5 \text{ sixes}} + \greenD{3\text{ sixes}}$

options.alt: An array of 48 circles arranged in 8 rows of 6 circles each. The circles in the first 5 rows are shaded blue. The circles in the next 3 rows are shaded green. A curved bracket across the top of the array is labeled 6. A curved bracket on the left side of the array is labeled 8.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the array to break $6\times4$ into two groups.**


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The array shows $6\times4$, or  $6$ fours.

Let's use the array to break the $6$ fours into two parts.

The dotted line breaks up the $6$ rows into $\blueD3$ rows and $\greenD3$ rows.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Above the line, there are $\blueD{3 \text{ rows of }4{ \text{ dots}}}$. 


[[☃ image 1]]

Below the line, there are $\greenD{3 \text{ rows of }4{ \text{ dots}}}$. 


[[☃ image 1]]
$6\times4=\blueD{3 \text{ fours}} + \greenD{3\text{ fours}}$

options.alt: An array of 24 circles arranged in 6 rows of 4 circles each. The circles in the first 3 rows are shaded blue. The circles in the last 3 rows are shaded green. A curved bracket across the top of the array is labeled 4. A curved bracket on the left side of the array is labeled 6.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the tape diagram to break $8\times4$ into two groups.**


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The tape diagram shows $8\times4$, or  $8{\text{ fours}}$.

Let's use the tape diagram to break the $8{\text{ fours}}$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks up the $8$ equal groups into $\purpleC5$ groups and $\greenD3$ groups.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The left side has $\purpleC{5 \text{ fours}}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The right side has $\greenD{3 \text{ fours}}$. 


[[☃ image 1]]
$8\times4=\purpleC{5 \text{ fours}} + \greenD{3\text{ fours}}$

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 8 equal parts. Each part is labeled 4. A curved bracket above the tape diagram is labeled 8 fours. The first 5 parts are shaded purple and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark. The last 3 parts are shaded green and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the tape diagram to break $7\times8$ into two groups.**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The tape diagram shows $7\times8$, or  $7{\text{ eights}}$.

Let's use the tape diagram to break the $7{\text{ eights}}$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks up the $7$ equal groups into $\purpleC5$ groups and $\greenD2$ groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The left side has $\purpleC{5 \text{ eights}}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The right side has $\greenD{2 \text{ eights}}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]
$7\times8=\purpleC{5 \text{ eights}} + \greenD{2\text{ eights}}$

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 7 equal parts. Each part is labeled 8. A curved bracket above the tape diagram is labeled 7 eights. The first 5 parts are shaded purple and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark. The last 2 parts are shaded green and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the tape diagram to break $9\times6$ into two groups.**



[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The tape diagram shows $9\times6$, or  $9{\text{ sixes}}$.

Let's use the tape diagram to break the $9{\text{ sixes}}$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks up the $9$ equal groups into $\purpleC5$ groups and $\greenD4$ groups.


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ explanation 1]]

The left side has $\purpleC{5 \text{ sixes}}$. 


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

The right side has $\greenD{4 \text{ sixes}}$.
$9\times6=\purpleC{5 \text{ sixes}} + \greenD{4\text{ sixes}}$

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 9 equal parts. Each part is labeled 6. A curved bracket above the tape diagram is labeled 9 sixes. The first 5 parts are shaded purple and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark. The last 4 parts are shaded green and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate.**  
*Hint: Use the image to help break $7\times4$ up into two expressions.*


[[☃ image 1]]

||
-:|:-
$7\times 4 $|$= \purpleD(~$[[☃ numeric-input 4]]$\purpleD{{} \times 4)}+\maroonD{(2 \times 4)}$|
hints:
The tape diagram shows $7\times4$.

Let's use the tape diagram to break the $7\times4$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks up $7\times4$ into $\purpleD{5\times4}$ and $\maroonD{2\times4}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
${7} \times {4} =(\purpleD{5\times4}) + (\maroonD{2\times4})$

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 7 equal parts. Each part is labeled 4. A curved bracket above the tape diagram is labeled 7 times 4. The first 5 parts are shaded purple and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark. The last 2 parts are shaded green and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Use the image to help break $7\times6$ up into two expressions.*

[[☃ image 1]]

||
-:|:-
$7\times 6$|$= \purpleE(~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$\purpleE{ {}\times 6)}+\maroonE{(2 \times 6)}$|
hints:
The tape diagram shows $7\times6$.

Let's use the tape diagram to break the $7\times6$ into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks up $7\times6$ into $\purpleE{5\times6}$ and $\maroonE{2\times6}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
${7} \times {6} =(\purpleE{5\times6}) + (\maroonE{2\times6})$

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 7 equal parts. Each part is labeled 6. A curved bracket above the tape diagram is labeled 7 times 6. The first 5 parts are shaded purple and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark. The last 2 parts are shaded maroon and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Use the image to help break $8\times4$ up into two expressions.*

[[☃ image 1]]

||
-:|:-
$8\times 4 $|$= \purpleE{5\text{ fours}}~ +$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]] $\maroonE{\text{ fours}}$|
hints:
The tape diagram shows $8\times4$, or  $8$ fours.

Let's use the tape diagram to break the $8$ fours into two parts.

The tape diagram breaks up $8$ fours into $\purpleE5$ fours and $\maroonE3$ fours.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$8\times4=\purpleE{5 \text{ fours}} + \maroonE{3\text{ fours}}$

options.alt: A tape diagram divided into 8 equal parts. Each part is labeled 4. A curved bracket above the tape diagram is labeled 8 fours. The first 5 parts are shaded purple and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark. The last 3 parts are shaded green and a curved bracket below them is labeled with a question mark.

*Hint: Use the image to help break $9\times4$ up into two expressions.*

[[☃ image 1]]

||
-:|:-
$9\times 4 $|$= \purpleD{5\text{ fours}}~ +$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]] $\maroonD{\text{ fours}}$|
hints:
The array shows $9\times4$, or  $9$ fours.

Let's use the array to break the $9$ fours into two parts.

The dotted line breaks up $9$ fours into $\purpleD{5}$ fours and $\maroonD4$ fours.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$9\times4=\purpleD{5 \text{ fours}} + \maroonD{4\text{ fours}}$

options.alt: An array of 36 circles arranged in 9 rows of 4 circles each. The circles in the first 5 rows are shaded purple. The circles in the next 4 rows are shaded maroon. A curved bracket across the top of the array is labeled 4. A curved bracket on the left side of the array is labeled 9.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Use the image to help break $8\times6$ up into two expressions.*

[[☃ image 1]]

||
-:|:-
$8\times 6 $|$= \purpleD(~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]${}\purpleD{ \times 6)}+\maroonE{(3 \times 6)}$|
hints:
The array shows $8\times6$.

Let's use the array to break the $8$ sixes into two parts.

The dotted line breaks up $8\times6$ into $\purpleD{5\times6}$ and $\maroonE{3\times6}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 2]]
${8} \times {6} =(\purpleD{5\times6}) + (\maroonE{3\times6})$

options.alt: An array of 48 circles arranged in 6 rows of 8 circles each. The circles in the left 5 columns are shaded purple. The circles in the right 3 columns are shaded maroon. A dotted line separates the left 5 columns from the right 3 columns. A curved bracket across the top of the array is labeled 8. A curved bracket on the left side of the array is labeled 6.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equation.**  
*Hint: Use the image to help break $9\times8$ up into two expressions.*



[[☃ image 1]]

||
-:|:-
$9\times 8 $|$= \maroonD(~$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]${}\maroonD{ \times 8)}+\purpleE{(4 \times 8)}$|
hints:
The array shows $9\times8$.

Let's use the array to break the $9$ eights into two parts.

The dotted line breaks up $9\times8$ into $\maroonD{5\times8}$ and $\purpleE{4\times8}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 2]]
${9} \times {8} =(\maroonD{5\times8}) + (\purpleE{4\times8})$

options.alt: An array of 72 circles arranged in 9 rows of 8 circles each. The circles in the first 5 rows are shaded maroon. The circles in the next 4 rows are shaded purple. A curved bracket across the top of the array is labeled 8. A curved bracket to the left of the array is labeled 9.
